prospects.trigger: Luma is expanding with a new AI-powered production studio for high-profile media projects.
prospects.brandWeakness: The brand launch lacks a detailed visual identity for their AI-focused initiative online.
prospects.notes: Luma's new project suggests interest in digital enhancements and visual storytelling, fitting for advanced web solutions.

scraped.homepage: AgentRQ | Human-in-the-Loop Agent Task Management for Claude & ACP Agents Human-in-the-Loop Agent Task Management for Claude & ACP Agents AgentRQ How It Works Features Docs Blog Pricing Sign In Get Started Free Real-Time Agent-Human Task Collaboration Agents & Humans Collaborate In Real Time — via MCP, Zero Friction AgentRQ is the real-time agent-human task collaboration platform built for Claude Code and ACP-compatible agents (like Gemini). Agents assign tasks to you. You assign tasks back to agents. Everyone moves forward simultaneously. One workspace per agent. Multiple workspaces = multiple agents running in parallel. Connect in 60 seconds and manage Claude, Gemini, and other ACP agents from a single dashboard. Start Free — No Credit Card See How It Works AgentRQ Workspace — Tasks Backend Agent v2 workspace #a8f2 Review PR #142 — auth refactor ONGOING Approve DB migration script BLOCKED Update API documentation NOT STARTED → Agent: run lint + type-check NEW ⏱ Daily report — runs at 09:00 SCHEDULED 4 Tasks — 1 needs your input Live Live simulation — actual AgentRQ workspace — Tasks Created — Users Signed Up — Messages Sent — Approvals Claude Code Only Designed Around Claude Code AgentRQ is built specifically for Claude Code's MCP channel protocol. Agents and humans exchange tasks and decisions through Claude's native <channel> primitive — structured collaboration with no polling, no webhooks, no custom SDK. Claude Code CLI-based coding agent running in your terminal MCP Channel Native Claude notification protocol — zero config You, Anywhere Reply from any browser — phone, tablet, desktop Agents and humans, working together Not a notification tool. A collaboration platform — tasks flow both ways, across every workspace you run. Fragmented Without AgentRQ One agent, one task, one thread — serial work only Agent needs input — no structured way to ask Human can't delegate back to agent either Parallel work across teams impossible Collaborative The AgentRQ Way Bidirectional task flow: Agent assigns tasks to human — with full context Human assigns tasks back to agent — instantly Both sides reply, update, and move forward Multiple workspaces in parallel: Marketing agent · Sales agent · Coding agent Each workspace isolated — 1 agent per workspace One dashboard to manage all of them Setup in 60 Seconds How AgentRQ Works Three steps from zero to a fully connected agent-human collaboration workspace. 1 Create a Workspace Sign up, create a workspace, and get your unique MCP connection URL. Takes 30 seconds. app.agentrq.com/ws/ YOUR_ID 2 Connect Your Agent Add AgentRQ to your .mcp.json . Claude Code is natively supported, or use ACP Gateway to connect Gemini and other agents. # For Gemini & ACP agents: npx @agentrq/acp-gateway \ -- gemini --acp 3 Collaborate in Real-Time Agent creates tasks, notifies you instantly. You reply from the dashboard — agent resumes in seconds. ✓ Approved. Agent resumes with your input — no context lost. Workspaces & Task Management Each workspace connects one Claude agent to your team. Create a workspace for coding, another for marketing, another for sales — each agent works independently in parallel. One dashboard, full visibility across all of them. ✓ Task Lifecycle Tracking Tasks flow through notstarted → ongoing → blocked → done . Agents and humans both update status in real-time. ✓ Attachments & Context Agents attach files, screenshots, and structured data to tasks. Humans see the full context before responding. AgentRQ Workspace — Tasks Review PR #142 — auth refactor ONGOING Approve DB migration script BLOCKED Deploy to staging env DONE Update API documentation NOT STARTED 4 Tasks — 1 needs your input Live .mcp.json — Claude Code Config // Add to your project's .mcp.json { "mcpServers" : { "agentrq" : { "type" : "http" , "url" : "https://WORKSPACE_ID.mcp.agentrq.com/mcp?token=TOKEN" } } } // Then start Claude: $ claude --dangerously-load-development-channels \ server:agentrq [MCP] agentrq channel connected ✓ [MCP] Tools: createTask, reply, updateTaskStatus, getWorkspace MCP-Native Agent Integration AgentRQ exposes a standard MCP server per workspace, designed for Claude Code's notification channel system. No SDKs to install, no webhooks to configure. Claude gets four powerful tools the moment it connects. createTask Assign work to the human operator with full context reply Send progress updates and messages in real-time updateTaskStatus Move tasks through the workflow lifecycle getWorkspace Read workspace state, mission, and task history Bring Your Own Agent (ACP) Not using Claude Code? No problem. AgentRQ supports any agent that speaks the Agent Client Protocol (ACP) . Using our open-source ACP Gateway , you can connect Gemini CLI and other compatible agents to your AgentRQ workspaces. ✓ Gemini CLI Support Run acp-gateway -- gemini --acp to bridge Gemini's power with AgentRQ's task management. ✓ Open Protocol ACP is the emerging standard for agent-client communication. AgentRQ is at the forefront of this ecosystem. Setup in 60 Seconds

How AgentRQ Works

Three steps from zero to a fully connected agent-human collaboration workspace.

1

Create a Workspace

Sign up, create a workspace, and get your unique MCP connection URL. Takes 30 seconds.

app.agentrq.com/ws/YOUR_ID
2

Connect Your Agent

Add AgentRQ to your .mcp.json. Claude Code is natively supported, or use ACP Gateway to connect Gemini and other agents. 

# For Gemini & ACP agents:
npx @agentrq/acp-gateway \
  -- gemini --acp
3

Collaborate in Real-Time

Agent creates tasks, notifies you instantly. You reply from the dashboard — agent resumes in seconds.

✓ Approved. Agent resumes with your input — no context lost.

Workspaces & Task Management

Each workspace connects one Claude agent to your team. Create a workspace for coding, another for marketing, another for sales — each agent works independently in parallel. One dashboard, full visibility across all of them.

  • ✓
    Task Lifecycle Tracking

    Tasks flow through notstarted → ongoing → blocked → done. Agents and humans both update status in real-time.

  • ✓
    Attachments & Context

    Agents attach files, screenshots, and structured data to tasks. Humans see the full context before responding.

MCP-Native Agent Integration

AgentRQ exposes a standard MCP server per workspace, designed for Claude Code's notification channel system. No SDKs to install, no webhooks to configure. Claude gets four powerful tools the moment it connects.

createTask

Assign work to the human operator with full context

reply

Send progress updates and messages in real-time

updateTaskStatus

Move tasks through the workflow lifecycle

getWorkspace

Read workspace state, mission, and task history

Bring Your Own Agent (ACP)

Not using Claude Code? No problem. AgentRQ supports any agent that speaks the Agent Client Protocol (ACP). Using our open-source ACP Gateway, you can connect Gemini CLI and other compatible agents to your AgentRQ workspaces.

  • ✓
    Gemini CLI Support

    Run acp-gateway -- gemini --acp to bridge Gemini's power with AgentRQ's task management.

  • ✓
    Open Protocol

    ACP is the emerging standard for agent-client communication. AgentRQ is at the forefront of this ecosystem.

Connect any agent
Run via npx
$ npx @agentrq/acp-gateway \
    -- gemini --acp

# ACP Gateway Bridge Active
# Connected to workspace: #a8f2
# Ready for tasks...
Read ACP Setup Guide →

Real-Time Agent-Human Task Collaboration

AgentRQ is not a notification tool. It's a structured collaboration layer — agents and humans exchange tasks, context, and decisions in real time through Claude's native MCP channel protocol.

  • 1
    Agent → Human Task Assignment

    Agent creates a task with full context, attachments, and a question. It arrives as a <channel> message in Claude Code — sub-second, no polling.

  • 2
    Human → Agent Task Assignment

    You reply or delegate a new task back — from the dashboard, on any device. The agent receives it instantly and resumes with full context, no re-prompting.

  • 3
    Full Thread History Per Task

    Every task has its own message thread — decisions, replies, and context all persisted. Review the complete collaboration history at any time.

# The ROI of Premium Web Design for Startups: A Deep Dive into Revenue and Conversion

Investing in premium web design for startups isn't just a trend—it's a necessity if you're serious about scaling. I've seen it firsthand. A well-crafted, premium web design sets the tone and can significantly affect your startup's credibility and customer perception. From our experience at Brandivibe, clients see notable differences in engagement when they opt for high-end, immersive designs. Think of how Stripe and Linear look so different from their competitors. That's not by accident—it's strategic and intentional.

## Quantifying Revenue Impact

Let's break it down. If your average order value sits around $200 and your monthly traffic is 10,000 visitors, a standard 2% conversion rate yields $40,000 revenue. But introduce a premium design, and we've seen conversion rates soar to 3% or more, driving revenue to $60,000. That's a $20K difference monthly. Over a year, that's an extra $240,000 in your pocket.

After implementing a 3D interactive site that not only looked stunning but was deeply intuitive—we brought that down to 42%. It's not just about aesthetics; it's about guiding users effortlessly through their journey.

# Why Your Startup Homepage is a Strategic Advantage

Your homepage is not just a digital business card. It's your startup's frontline in a competitive market. If you're treating it like a static brochure, you're missing the chance to convert visitors into believers. I’ve seen startups transform their trajectory by treating their homepage as a strategic asset. Let's dig into why and how you should do the same.

## The Power of a Strategic Homepage

I once worked with a promising fintech company that had an incredible product but was struggling with user acquisition. Their homepage was generic—a design afterthought. We redefined it as a strategic tool, a narrative that led visitors by the hand and showcased their unique value proposition. Suddenly, their conversion rates soared. That's the power of a strategic homepage.

## Why Traditional Homepages Fail in Competitive Markets

In competitive sectors, a traditional homepage—one that follows outdated templates and focuses only on aesthetics—simply won't cut it. Users have learned to tune out noise, and a generic site is just that: noise. Instead, the homepage should act as a guide, steering potential customers through your brand story and capabilities. According to the Nielsen Norman Group, users form an impression of a website in about 50 milliseconds. If your homepage doesn't immediately communicate strategic intent, you've lost them.

## Crafting a Homepage with Purpose

A purposeful homepage aligns tightly with business goals. It isn't just about pretty pixels. It's about guiding users towards specific actions: signing up, subscribing, or contacting sales. A Series A SaaS we worked with experienced this firsthand. Their conversion-optimized homepage led users through a clear journey with succinct messaging and interactive elements.

## Real-World Examples of Strategic Homepages

Consider Stripe's homepage—it doesn’t drown you in information. Instead, it strategically leads with their most compelling offers and uses interactive elements to keep you engaged. [Helix](/helix) and [Pulse](/pulse) from our demo pages also demonstrate how a homepage can narrate a brand story engagingly—without overwhelming the user.

## The Metrics Behind a High-Impact Homepage

If you're wondering whether your homepage is doing its job, pay attention to metrics. Bounce rate, time on page, and conversion rates are the trifecta. A homepage that strategically engages will show lower bounce rates and higher conversions. From our experience at Brandivibe, we’ve seen bounce rates drop by over 20% after a strategic redesign.

| Metric         | Before Redesign | After Redesign |
|----------------|-----------------|----------------|
| Bounce Rate    | 70%             | 50%            |
| Time on Page   | 1:30 minutes    | 3:00 minutes   |
| Conversion Rate| 1.5%            | 4%             |

## How Brandivibe Elevates Startup Homepages

At Brandivibe, we infuse every homepage with strategic intent. Whether it’s through [Neuron's](/neuron) immersive 3D elements or [Axiom's](/axiom) sophisticated interaction design, we ensure your homepage isn’t just seen, but remembered. Our approach is tailored to convert interest into action, leveraging unique web experiences that captivate users.

## Your Homepage as a Competitive Lever

In a world where first impressions are formed in the blink of an eye, your startup's homepage should be more than a digital relic. It should be a living, breathing part of your business strategy. Every pixel, every line of text should serve a purpose—driving user engagement and business growth. If you're serious about standing out, it's time your homepage reflects that.

Want to see how your homepage can become a strategic advantage? Let’s talk.

# Why Cheap Websites Kill Premium Opportunities for Startups

Ever wondered why your competitor's $15K website outperforms your $3K template? From my experience, it's not just about aesthetics — it's about perception. Your startup's web presence is a digital handshake, and in the world of premium web design for startups, a weak grip sends the wrong message entirely.

## The Hidden Costs of Cheap Web Design

I've seen too many founders fall into the trap of saving upfront on web design, only to pay dearly in lost opportunities. If your website looks cheap, it signals to premium buyers that your business might lack the sophistication or reliability they expect. And believe me, that's a hard bell to unring.

## Cheap Design Signals Low Status: How It Impacts Perception

When a site looks like it was cobbled together on a shoestring, it screams low status. I've watched potential clients navigate away from pages that felt outdated or unprofessional. An investor or high-ticket customer scrolling through your site doesn't want a reason to raise an eyebrow.

## Technical Failures That Repel Visitors

Downtimes, slow load speeds, and broken links — these are hallmarks of budget web design. Google research shows just a 1-second delay in mobile load times can reduce conversions by up to 20%. That's your audience hitting the back button. Expensive mistake.

## Design Mistakes That Hurt Conversions

Flashing banners, cluttered layouts, and a lack of interactive elements might save you a buck initially, but they destroy user engagement. On one client's site, we replaced a chaotic homepage with a [neuron](/neuron) inspired design, focusing on immersive, interactive storytelling — their bounce rate dropped by 40%.
